name: Centro Budista Tibetano Dag Shang Kagyu
description: Dag Shang Kagyu is a Tibetan Buddhist center in the Kagyu tradition serving the Buddhist community of Buenos Aires. It offers meditation classes, dharma teachings, and retreat programs, with visiting teachers from the Kagyu lineage. The center provides one of the few opportunities for Tibetan Buddhist practice in Argentina.

name: Jardim Zen Buenos Aires
description: Asociacion Zen de America Latina is one of the oldest Zen centers in South America, founded in the Soto tradition. Located in Buenos Aires, it offers daily zazen, sesshins, and dharma study in Spanish. The center has played a pioneering role in establishing Zen practice throughout the Southern Cone.

name: SGI Argentina
description: SGI Argentina is the Soka Gakkai International center in Buenos Aires, part of the worldwide Nichiren Buddhist lay movement. Argentina has one of the largest SGI communities in Latin America, with thousands of members across the country. The center offers daily practice meetings, study groups, and cultural activities promoting peace and education.

name: Mosteiro Zen Morro da Vargem Zenkoji
Mosteiro Zen Morro da Vargem
address: Morro da Vargem, Ibiracu, ES 29670-000, Brazil
description: Mosteiro Zen Morro da Vargem is the first and oldest Zen Buddhist monastery in Latin America, established in 1974 by a Japanese Soto Zen mission. Located on a hilltop in the state of Espirito Santo, it offers residential training, sesshin retreats, and meditation instruction. The monastery maintains a traditional Japanese-style zendo amid Atlantic Forest vegetation.

description: Centro de Dharma da Paz is a Kagyu lineage Tibetan Buddhist center in Rio de Janeiro, offering meditation instruction, dharma teachings, and retreat programs. Located in the scenic Cosme Velho neighborhood near the Christ the Redeemer statue, it serves as a major Tibetan Buddhist hub in southeastern Brazil. The center hosts visiting lamas and teachers from the Kagyu tradition.

description: BSGI is the headquarters of Soka Gakkai International in Brazil, which has the largest SGI membership outside Japan with over 150,000 practitioners. Located in the Liberdade neighborhood (Sao Paulo's historic Japanese quarter), it serves as a hub for Nichiren Buddhist practice, cultural activities, and peace education. Brazil's SGI movement has been active since the 1960s.

description: Daissen Ji is a prominent Soto Zen temple in Sao Paulo founded by Roshi Tokuda, one of Brazil's most influential Zen teachers. The temple offers daily zazen, sesshins, and dharma talks in Portuguese. It has been instrumental in establishing a distinctly Brazilian Zen practice community.

name: Templo Hompoji
address: Rua Sao Joaquim 285, Liberdade, Sao Paulo, SP 01508-001, Brazil
description: Templo Hompoji is a Jodo Shinshu (Shin Buddhist) temple in Sao Paulo's historic Japanese Liberdade district. Established to serve the large Japanese-Brazilian community, it is one of the oldest Buddhist temples in South America. The temple offers regular services, dharma talks, and cultural events honoring the Japanese Buddhist heritage in Brazil.

name: Templo Honpa Hongwanji
address: Rua Padre Albino Bareta, Mogi das Cruzes

name: Templo Memorial da Brasil Soka Gakkai Internacional

name: Templo Zu Lai
address: Estrada Municipal Fernando Nobre 1461, Cotia, SP 06705-030, Brazil
description: Templo Zu Lai is the largest Buddhist temple in Latin America, a spectacular Fo Guang Shan complex in the hills west of Sao Paulo. Featuring traditional Chinese architecture, extensive gardens, and a large Buddha hall, it serves as the South American headquarters of Fo Guang Shan. The temple offers meditation classes, cultural programs, and a renowned vegetarian restaurant.

name: Chapin Mill Retreat Center
description: Calgary Zen Centre offers regular zazen, sesshins, and dharma study programs in the tradition of Shunryu Suzuki Roshi. Serving practitioners in southern Alberta, it provides one of the few dedicated Zen practice spaces in the Canadian prairies. The centre welcomes beginners and experienced practitioners alike.

name: Gaden Samten Ling Tibetan Buddhist Meditation Society
Jam Tse Cho Ling Tibetan Buddhist Temple, Calgary
Birken Forest Buddhist Monastery
description: Birken Forest Buddhist Monastery is a Thai Forest Tradition monastery in the interior of British Columbia, founded by Ajahn Sona in the lineage of Ajahn Chah. Set amid forests and lakes, it supports a small monastic community following traditional Vinaya practice. The monastery offers meditation retreats and is entirely supported by donations.

name: Birken Forest Monastery

name: Chua Bao Lam
address: East 44th Avenue, Vancouver, British Columbia V5W

name: International Buddhist Temple
address: 9160 Steveston Hwy, Richmond, BC V7A 1M5, Canada
description: The International Buddhist Temple is one of the largest and most ornate Chinese Buddhist temples in North America, featuring classical Chinese palatial architecture. Located in Richmond near Vancouver, it serves a large Chinese Buddhist community and welcomes visitors of all backgrounds. The temple offers meditation classes, dharma talks, cultural events, and a renowned vegetarian restaurant.

name: Tung Lin Kok Yuen Canada Society
address: 2495 Victoria Dr, Vancouver, BC V5N 4K9, Canada
description: Tung Lin Kok Yuen is a Chinese Buddhist Pure Land temple in Vancouver serving the large Chinese Buddhist community in the Lower Mainland. The temple offers daily chanting, meditation, dharma classes, and traditional Buddhist ceremonies. It provides a welcoming environment for both Chinese-speaking and English-speaking practitioners.

name: Gampo Abbey
address: 1533 Gampo Abbey Rd, Pleasant Bay, NS B0E 2P0, Canada
description: Gampo Abbey is a Shambhala Buddhist monastery on Cape Breton Island, founded by Chogyam Trungpa Rinpoche. It became widely known through resident teacher Pema Chodron's bestselling books on Buddhist practice. The monastery offers group and solitary retreat programs in a dramatic coastal setting overlooking the Gulf of St. Lawrence.

name: Arrow River Forest Hermitage
address: R.R. #5, Thunder Bay, ON P7C 5N3, Canada
description: Arrow River Forest Hermitage is a secluded Thai Forest Tradition monastery in northwestern Ontario, offering a quiet environment for monastic training and lay meditation practice. The hermitage supports a small community of monks following the Vinaya in the Ajahn Chah lineage. It offers periodic retreats and welcomes visitors for short stays.

name: Avalokiteshvara Kadampa Buddhist Centre
address: Wyndham Street North, Guelph, Ontario N1H 8N9

name: Chandrakirti Kadampa Meditation Centre
address: 642 Dupont St, Toronto, ON M6G 1Z5, Canada
description: Chandrakirti Kadampa Meditation Centre is the main New Kadampa Tradition centre in Canada, offering daily meditation classes, workshops, and retreats. Located in the Annex neighborhood of Toronto, it provides accessible Buddhist meditation instruction in the Gelug-derived Kadampa tradition. The centre hosts regional festivals and teacher training programs.

name: Fo Guang Shan Buddha's Light Centre Waterloo
Fo Guang Shan Temple of Ottawa
Fo Guang Shan Temple of Toronto
Soka Gakkai International Association of Canada
Tisarana Buddhist Monastery
description: Tisarana Buddhist Monastery is a Thai Forest Tradition monastery in eastern Ontario, founded by Ajahn Viradhammo, a senior Western disciple of Ajahn Chah. Located on 200 acres of forest and farmland, it supports a resident monastic community and offers meditation retreats and Sunday programs. The monastery operates entirely on the basis of generosity.

name: Toronto Zen Centre
address: 33 High Park Gardens, Toronto, ON M6R 1S8, Canada
description: Toronto Zen Centre is a Soto Zen practice community in the High Park neighborhood, offering daily zazen, sesshins, and Zen study programs. Founded in the White Plum lineage of Taizan Maezumi Roshi, it maintains a residential practice program and welcomes practitioners of all levels. The centre is one of the oldest established Zen groups in Canada.

name: West End Buddhist Temple and Meditation Centre
Montreal Zen Center
address: 824 Rue Atwater, Montreal, QC H4C 2G1, Canada
description: The Montreal Zen Center offers daily zazen, intensive retreats, and dharma study in both French and English. One of the oldest Zen centres in Quebec, it serves as a bilingual bridge between English and French-speaking Buddhist communities. The center provides an accessible urban practice environment in the heart of Montreal.

name: Centro Budista de Santiago
description: Centro Budista de Santiago is a Tibetan Buddhist center in the Kagyu tradition, one of the most active Buddhist communities in Chile. It offers meditation classes, dharma teachings, and retreat programs for practitioners of all levels. The center hosts visiting Kagyu teachers and maintains connections with the international Kagyu community.

name: SGI Chile
description: SGI Chile is the Soka Gakkai International center in Santiago, part of the worldwide Nichiren Buddhist lay movement. Chile has one of the most active SGI communities in South America, with practice groups across the country. The center offers daily practice, study meetings, and cultural events promoting peace and humanistic values.

name: Templo Budista Fo Guang Shan Chile
Centro Budista Tibetano Yamantaka
description: Centro Budista Tibetano Yamantaka is an FPMT-affiliated Tibetan Buddhist center in Bogota, offering meditation courses, philosophy classes, and retreats in the Gelug tradition. It is one of the most established Tibetan Buddhist centers in the Andean region and regularly hosts visiting lamas. The center provides instruction in both Spanish and English.

name: Comunidad Budista Soto Zen de Colombia
description: The Soto Zen Community of Colombia offers regular zazen and sesshin retreats in Bogota. As one of the few established Zen groups in the Andean region, it serves practitioners across Colombia and neighboring countries. The community maintains connections with Soto Zen teachers in Japan and Latin America.

name: Zen Center of Costa Rica
description: The Zen Center of Costa Rica is a small but active Soto Zen practice community in the capital, offering regular zazen and sesshin retreats. It serves as a gathering point for Buddhist practitioners in Central America and provides meditation instruction in Spanish. The center is part of the growing Buddhist presence in the region.

name: Soka Gakkai Republica Dominicana Kaikan Oriental
address: Calle C, Santo Domingo Este, Santo Domingo 11519

name: Comunidad Zen del Ecuador
description: Comunidad Zen del Ecuador is a Soto Zen practice community in Quito, offering regular zazen and periodic retreats. Situated at nearly 3,000 meters elevation in the Andes, it provides a unique high-altitude setting for Zen practice. The community serves practitioners across Ecuador and is part of the growing Zen movement in Latin America.

name: Centro Budista Kadampa Guatemala
description: Centro Budista Kadampa Guatemala is a New Kadampa Tradition meditation center in Guatemala City, offering regular classes, workshops, and retreats. It is one of the few established Buddhist centers in Central America and provides accessible meditation instruction in Spanish. The center hosts periodic visits from NKT teachers.

name: Casa Tibet Mexico
address: Av. Nuevo Leon 159, Hippodromo, Mexico City, CDMX 06100
description: Casa Tibet Mexico is the principal Tibetan Buddhist center in Latin America, personally established by the Dalai Lama. Located in the Condesa neighborhood of Mexico City, it offers meditation courses, philosophy classes, and retreats in the Gelug tradition. The center has been instrumental in bringing Tibetan Buddhism to the Spanish-speaking world.

name: Centro Budista de la Ciudad de Mexico
description: Centro Budista de la Ciudad de Mexico is a non-sectarian Buddhist meditation center offering classes and retreats from multiple Buddhist traditions. Located in the heart of Mexico City, it serves as an accessible introduction to Buddhist practice for Spanish-speaking practitioners. The center hosts teachers from various traditions including Zen, Tibetan, and Theravada.

name: Centro Zen de Mexico
address: Calle Sabino 157, Santa Maria la Ribera, Mexico City, CDMX
description: Centro Zen de Mexico is one of the oldest established Zen practice centers in Latin America, offering daily zazen and sesshin retreats in the Soto tradition. The center serves as a hub for Zen practitioners across Mexico and provides instruction in Spanish. It maintains connections with Japanese Soto Zen organizations.

name: Fo Guang Shan Mexico
description: Fo Guang Shan Mexico is a branch of the international Fo Guang Shan Buddhist order, promoting Humanistic Buddhism in the Spanish-speaking world. The center offers meditation classes, dharma talks, cultural events, and vegetarian cooking classes. It serves as the main Fo Guang Shan hub for all of Latin America.

name: Comunidad Dzogchen Mexico
description: Comunidad Dzogchen Mexico is a Dzogchen practice community in the mountains of Morelos, part of the international Dzogchen Community founded by Chogyal Namkhai Norbu. Located near the spiritual town of Tepoztlan, it offers retreats, teachings, and community practice in the Nyingma tradition. The center draws practitioners from across Latin America.

name: Centro Zen Sanbo Kyodan Lima
description: Centro Zen Lima is a Sanbo Kyodan lineage Zen practice group in Peru's capital, offering regular zazen and periodic sesshins. It is one of the few established Zen communities in the Andean region. The center provides meditation instruction in Spanish and welcomes beginners to Buddhist practice.

name: Abhayagiri Buddhist Monastery
description: Abhayagiri Buddhist Monastery is a Thai Forest Tradition monastery in the lineage of Ajahn Chah, founded by Ajahn Pasanno and Ajahn Amaro. Situated on 280 acres of meadows and forests in Mendocino County, it supports a community of bhikkhus (monks) in traditional Vinaya practice. The monastery is entirely donation-based and welcomes visitors for day visits and longer stays.

name: City of Ten Thousand Buddhas
description: The City of Ten Thousand Buddhas (CTTB) is a large Buddhist monastic community and educational institution founded by Venerable Master Hsuan Hua in 1976. Located on a 488-acre campus (a former state hospital), it houses monastics, operates Dharma Realm Buddhist University, and runs K-12 schools. The city maintains a strict vegetarian and ascetic practice environment.

name: Deer Park Monastery
description: Deer Park Monastery is a practice center in the Plum Village tradition of Thich Nhat Hanh, set on a dry chaparral hillside in southern California. It hosts a community of monks and nuns who offer mindfulness retreats for individuals, families, and young people. The monastery emphasizes engaged Buddhism, mindful living, and building beloved community.

Fo Guang Shan San Bao Temple

name: Green Gulch Farm Zen Center
description: Green Gulch Farm Zen Center is a residential Zen practice center and organic farm located in a coastal valley near Muir Beach. Part of the San Francisco Zen Center network, it integrates Zen practice with organic farming and environmental education. The center offers public programs, guest stays, and residential practice opportunities.

name: Hsi Lai Temple
description: Hsi Lai Temple is one of the largest Buddhist temples in the Western Hemisphere, affiliated with the Fo Guang Shan order founded by Venerable Master Hsing Yun. The sprawling complex covers 15 acres and features traditional Chinese architecture, cultural exhibits, and a renowned vegetarian restaurant. It serves as the North American headquarters of Fo Guang Shan and offers daily meditation, classes, and cultural events.

name: Koyasan Beikoku Betsuin of Los Angeles
Land of Calm Abiding (Tibetan Buddhist Retreat Center)
Long Beach Sagely Monastery
address: East Ocean Boulevard, Long Beach, CA 90803

name: Metta Forest Monastery (Wat Metta)
description: Metta Forest Monastery is a Thai Forest Tradition monastery led by Thanissaro Bhikkhu (Ajahn Geoff), one of the most prolific translators of the Pali Canon into English. Set on 160 acres of avocado groves and scrubland in San Diego County, it offers a quiet environment for monastic training and meditation practice. The monastery is run entirely on donations and does not charge for teachings.

name: Nichiren Shoshu Myohoji Temple
address: North Crescent Heights Boulevard, West Hollywood, CA 90046

name: SGI-USA Santa Ana Buddhist Center
address: East Warner Avenue, Santa Ana, CA 92705

name: San Francisco Zen Center (Beginner's Mind Temple)
description: San Francisco Zen Center is one of the largest and most influential Soto Zen communities outside Japan, founded by Shunryu Suzuki Roshi in 1962. The City Center at 300 Page Street offers daily zazen, dharma talks, classes, and residential practice programs. It is the urban hub of a three-center network including Tassajara and Green Gulch.

name: San Mateo Buddhist Temple
address: South Claremont Street, San Mateo, CA

name: Shasta Abbey
description: Shasta Abbey is a Soto Zen monastery in the Order of Buddhist Contemplatives, founded by Reverend Master Jiyu-Kennett in 1970. Located at the base of Mount Shasta, the monastery maintains a year-round schedule of meditation, study, and ceremonial practice. The community of monks follows a Western adaptation of Soto Zen monastic training.

name: Shingon Buddhist International Institute
description: The Shingon Buddhist International Institute in Fresno is one of the few Shingon (Japanese esoteric Buddhist) centers in the United States. It offers services, meditation instruction, and teachings in the Shingon tradition. The institute serves as an important resource for the Japanese-American Buddhist community in California's Central Valley.

name: Soka Gakkai International San Francisco Culture Center
Spirit Rock Meditation Center
address: 5000 Sir Francis Drake Blvd, Woodacre, CA 94973
description: Spirit Rock Meditation Center is a major vipassana and mindfulness center in the hills of West Marin County, co-founded by Jack Kornfield. Set on 412 acres of rolling grasslands and oak woodlands, it offers a wide range of daylong, residential, and online retreats. Spirit Rock is known for its inclusive approach and diverse community of lay teachers.

name: Suzuki-Roshi
address: Suzuki-Roshi Memorial Trail, Tassajara Hot Springs, California

name: Tassajara Zen Mountain Center
description: Tassajara Zen Mountain Center is the first Japanese Buddhist monastery established outside Asia, deep in the Ventana Wilderness of the Los Padres National Forest. Founded in 1967 as part of SFZC, it offers intensive monastic practice periods in winter and a guest season in summer. The center is also famous for its natural hot springs and vegetarian cuisine.

name: Thiền Viện Pháp Thuận (Dharma Meditation Temple)
Vajrapani Institute
description: Vajrapani Institute is an FPMT-affiliated Tibetan Buddhist retreat center nestled in the Santa Cruz Mountains. It offers a wide range of retreats, courses, and workshops rooted in the Gelug tradition. The center sits on 70 acres of redwood forest and provides a serene environment for practice and study.

address: Coldwater Canyon Avenue, North Hollywood, CA 91605

name: Zen Center of Los Angeles (ZCLA)
address: 923 S Normandie Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90006
description: The Zen Center of Los Angeles was founded by Taizan Maezumi Roshi in 1967 and has been a seminal institution in American Zen, producing numerous influential teachers. Located in the Koreatown/Normandie neighborhood, it offers daily zazen, sesshins, and residential practice programs. The center combines both Soto and Rinzai elements in its training.

name: Kadampa Meditation Center Colorado
description: Kadampa Meditation Center Colorado offers Buddhist meditation classes, workshops, and retreats in the New Kadampa Tradition. Located in Colorado Springs, it serves as a hub for meditation practitioners along the Front Range. The center provides accessible programs for beginners and more advanced Buddhist study and practice.

name: Open Heart Sangha
address: North Nevada Avenue, Colorado Springs, CO 80903

name: Shambhala Mountain Center
address: 4921 County Road 68C, Red Feather Lakes, CO 80545
description: Shambhala Mountain Center is a 600-acre contemplative retreat center in the Colorado Rockies, part of the Shambhala Buddhist tradition established by Chogyam Trungpa Rinpoche. It features the Great Stupa of Dharmakaya, one of the largest Buddhist stupas in North America. The center hosts year-round retreats, meditation programs, and family-friendly events.

name: Soka Gakkai International Buddhist Center
address: North Union Boulevard, Colorado Springs, CO 80918

name: Tara Mandala
description: Tara Mandala is a Nyingma-lineage retreat center founded by Lama Tsultrim Allione on 700 acres in the San Juan Mountains of southwestern Colorado. It features a three-story Tara Temple, retreat cabins, and extensive wilderness. The center is known for its Feeding Your Demons practice and offers a range of retreats emphasizing the feminine divine in Buddhism.

name: Zen Center of Denver
description: The Zen Center of Denver is a Soto Zen practice community offering daily zazen, retreats, and dharma study programs. Located in the Highland neighborhood, it provides a welcoming urban practice environment with both residential and non-residential training options. The center emphasizes bringing Zen practice into everyday life.

name: Do Ngak Kunphen Ling Tibetan Buddhist Center for Universal Peace
Hartford Karma Thegsum Chöling Buddhist Center
Soka Gakkai USA Washington DC Cultural Center
Eastern United Temple Foundation of Jacksonville, INC. Cambodian Buddhist Temple

address: 1928 S Dixie Hwy, West Palm Beach, FL 33401
description: The Padmasambhava Buddhist Center in West Palm Beach is a branch of the PBC network founded by Khenchen Palden Sherab Rinpoche and Khenpo Tsewang Dongyal Rinpoche. It offers regular meditation sessions, dharma teachings, and practice programs in the Nyingma tradition. The center provides one of the few Tibetan Buddhist practice opportunities in southern Florida.

name: SGI-USA Florida Nature and Culture Center
Tallahassee Chan Center
address: North Paul Russell Road, Tallahassee, FL 32301

name: Vipassana Meditation
address: Tampa Executive Airport Road, Tampa, FL 33610

name: Wat Buddharangsi of Miami
description: Wat Buddharangsi is a Thai Buddhist temple and cultural center in southern Miami-Dade County, featuring traditional Thai architecture and ornate decorations. The temple serves both the Thai community and interested visitors, offering meditation instruction, cultural events, and traditional Thai Buddhist ceremonies. Its elaborately decorated shrine room and grounds make it a popular cultural destination.

name: Wat Mongkolratanaram (Thai Temple of Tampa)
description: Wat Mongkolratanaram is a Thai Buddhist temple in Tampa famous for its Sunday morning food market, which draws hundreds of visitors weekly for authentic Thai cuisine. The temple serves the local Thai community and offers meditation classes, Buddhist ceremonies, and cultural events. Its ornate Thai-style architecture and welcoming atmosphere make it a cultural landmark in the Tampa Bay area.

name: Georgia Buddhist Vihara
description: Georgia Buddhist Vihara is a Sri Lankan Theravada temple and meditation center serving the Buddhist community in the greater Atlanta area. The vihara offers regular meditation sessions, dhamma talks, Sunday school for children, and traditional Sri Lankan Buddhist ceremonies. It serves as a cultural and spiritual home for the Sri Lankan diaspora in the southeastern United States.

name: Chozen-ji (International Zen Dojo)
description: Chozen-ji is a Rinzai Zen temple and training center in Honolulu founded by Omori Sogen Roshi, uniquely integrating Zen practice with Japanese martial and fine arts. The temple sits on 9 acres in Kalihi Valley and features traditional Japanese architecture, gardens, and a dojo. Training includes zazen, koan study, swordsmanship, calligraphy, and tea ceremony.

name: Hsu Yun Temple - Chinese Buddhist Association
Mantokuji Soto Zen Temple of Maui
Nechung Dorje Drayang Ling, Wood Valley Temple & Retreat
Ryumonji Zen Monastery
description: Ryumonji Zen Monastery is a Soto Zen monastery in the Katagiri lineage, situated on 100 acres of rolling farmland in northeast Iowa. Founded by Shoken Winecoff, it offers year-round residential training, sesshin retreats, and practice periods. The monastery maintains an active organic garden and integrates work practice into its training schedule.

name: Midwest Buddhist Temple
description: Midwest Buddhist Temple is a Jodo Shinshu (Shin Buddhist) temple in the Lincoln Park neighborhood of Chicago, affiliated with the Buddhist Churches of America. Established in 1945, it serves the Japanese-American community and anyone interested in Shin Buddhism. The temple is known for its annual Ginza Holiday festival celebrating Japanese culture.

address: 901 Furnace Mountain Rd, Clay City, KY 40312
description: Furnace Mountain is a Zen retreat center in the Red River Gorge area of Kentucky, affiliated with the Kwan Um School of Zen. Set on 600 acres of Appalachian forest, it offers sesshin retreats, solitary cabin retreats, and intensive practice periods. The center provides a rare opportunity for Zen practice in the rural South.

name: Insight Meditation Society
description: The Insight Meditation Society (IMS) is one of the most influential Western vipassana centers, co-founded by Sharon Salzberg, Jack Kornfield, and Joseph Goldstein in 1975. It operates two facilities: the Retreat Center offering structured group retreats, and the Forest Refuge for longer self-guided practice. IMS has been instrumental in bringing mindfulness and vipassana meditation to the West.

name: Hokyoji Zen Practice Community
description: Hokyoji Zen Practice Community is a rural Soto Zen retreat center in southeastern Minnesota, set amid bluffs and hardwood forests along the Root River. The center offers sesshin retreats, work practice periods, and solitary cabin retreats in the Katagiri Roshi lineage. Its remote, natural setting provides an ideal environment for intensive zazen practice.

name: Wat Promwachirayan

Magnolia Grove Monastery
description: Magnolia Grove Monastery is the third Plum Village practice center established in the United States, founded by Thich Nhat Hanh in the Mississippi Delta region. The monastery has a community of monks and nuns who offer mindfulness retreats and Days of Mindfulness throughout the year. It provides a rare Buddhist practice opportunity in the Deep South.

name: Ewam Buddhist Institute
description: Ewam Buddhist Institute, also known as the Garden of One Thousand Buddhas, is a Sakya tradition center founded by Gochen Tulku Sang-ngag Rinpoche. Located on the Flathead Indian Reservation in western Montana, it features a remarkable outdoor garden with 1,000 Buddha statues. The institute offers teachings, retreats, and community events.

name: Chùa Minh Đức
address: Wiley Davis Road, Greensboro, North Carolina 27407

name: Fo Guang Shan North Carolina Temple
Southern Dharma Retreat Center
description: Southern Dharma Retreat Center is a non-sectarian contemplative retreat center in the Appalachian Mountains of western North Carolina, offering retreats from a variety of Buddhist traditions and other contemplative paths. Set on 40 acres near the Appalachian Trail, it provides an affordable retreat environment in the southeastern United States. Programs include vipassana, Zen, Tibetan, and non-dual teachings.

name: Linh Quang Buddhist Center
address: West Pleasant Hill Road, Lincoln, NE 68523

name: Nebraska Zen Center (Heartland Temple)
description: Nebraska Zen Center, also known as Heartland Temple, is a Soto Zen practice community in the Katagiri Roshi lineage, serving practitioners in the Great Plains region. Located in a residential Omaha neighborhood, it offers daily zazen, sesshins, and classes on Zen practice and Buddhist texts. The center provides one of the few dedicated Zen practice spaces in the central United States.

name: Pine Wind Zen Monastery
address: McKendimen Road, Medford Township, New Jersey 08088

name: SGI-USA Buddhist Community Center
address: Greenbriar Road, Cherry Hill Township, New Jersey 08034

name: Bodhi Manda Zen Center
description: Bodhi Manda Zen Center is a Rinzai Zen monastery and hot springs retreat center located in the Jemez Mountains of New Mexico, founded by Joshu Sasaki Roshi. The center features natural hot springs, a traditional Japanese-style zendo, and offers intensive sesshin retreats. Its stunning high-desert and mountain setting provides an ideal environment for deep practice.

name: Upaya Zen Center
address: 1404 Cerro Gordo Rd, Santa Fe, NM 87501
description: Upaya Zen Center is a Buddhist center and residential community founded by Roshi Joan Halifax, known for its integration of Zen practice with social engagement, end-of-life care, and neuroscience. Located at the base of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains in Santa Fe, it offers residential programs, professional trainings, and retreats. Upaya is a leader in contemplative end-of-life care education.

name: Amitabha Buddhist Society
address: Main Street, New York, New York 11435

name: Blue Cliff Monastery
description: Blue Cliff Monastery is a Plum Village tradition practice center in the Hudson Valley, home to a community of monks and nuns in the lineage of Thich Nhat Hanh. Set on 80 acres of woodland, it offers regular Days of Mindfulness, week-long retreats, and Wake Up programs for young adults. The monastery provides a welcoming environment for practitioners of all backgrounds.

name: Brooklyn Zen Center
address: 505 Carroll Street, New York, New York 11215

address: East Williston Avenue, Williston Park, NY 11596

address: West 26th Street, New York, New York 10001

name: Chuang Yen Monastery
description: Chuang Yen Monastery, operated by the Buddhist Association of the United States, houses the largest indoor statue of the Buddha in the Western Hemisphere, a 37-foot Vairocana Buddha in the Great Buddha Hall. Set on 225 acres in Putnam County, the monastery offers meditation retreats, dharma talks, and cultural programs. It is a major pilgrimage site for Chinese Buddhists in America.

name: Dharma Drum Retreat Center
description: Dharma Drum Retreat Center is the North American headquarters of the Dharma Drum Mountain organization founded by Master Sheng Yen. Set on 125 acres in the foothills of the Shawangunk Mountains, it offers Chan meditation retreats including intensive seven-day silent retreats. The center is known for its elegant architecture blending Chinese and contemporary styles.

name: Han Ma Um Zen Center of New York
Kadampa Meditation Center New York
description: Kadampa Meditation Center New York is the mother center of the New Kadampa Tradition in the US, set on 82 acres of woodland in the Catskill Mountains region. The center features a traditional Buddhist temple and offers a full range of meditation retreats, teacher training programs, and festival events. It draws practitioners from throughout the Northeast for weekend and week-long courses.

name: Kagyu Thubten Choling
description: Kagyu Thubten Choling is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery and retreat center founded by Lama Norlha Rinpoche in the Karma Kagyu lineage. Set on over 80 acres in the Hudson Valley, the monastery supports both monastic community and lay practitioners. It is known for its traditional three-year, three-month retreat program.

name: Karma Triyana Dharmachakra Monastery
address: Meads Mountain Road, Town of Woodstock, New York 12498

name: Namgyal Monastery
description: Namgyal Monastery is the North American seat of the personal monastery of the Dalai Lama, originally founded in Dharamsala, India. It offers extensive programs in Tibetan Buddhist philosophy, meditation, and ritual arts. The monastery is home to resident monks and serves as a vibrant center for both monastic and lay practitioners.

name: New York Buddhist Vihara
address: 214-22 Spencer Avenue, New York, New York 11427

name: Rochester Zen Center
description: Rochester Zen Center is one of the oldest Zen centers in America, founded by Philip Kapleau Roshi, author of 'The Three Pillars of Zen.' The center offers a rigorous practice schedule with daily zazen, sesshin retreats, and dokusan (private teacher interviews). It maintains a residential community and a comprehensive training program.

name: Won Dharma Center
description: Won Dharma Center is a retreat and meditation center of Won Buddhism, a modern reform tradition from Korea, set on 460 acres in the Hudson Valley. The architecturally striking center features a round meditation hall and modern guest facilities. It offers meditation retreats, mindfulness programs, and interfaith events throughout the year.

name: World Buddhist Chan Jing Center Inc
address: 3583 Weed Road, Pine Bush, New York 12566

name: Zen Mountain Monastery
description: Zen Mountain Monastery is the main residential center of the Mountains and Rivers Order, founded by John Daido Loori Roshi in the Catskill Mountains. The monastery offers a comprehensive training program that includes zazen, koan study, liturgy, art practice, and body practice. Set on 230 acres of forested land, it is known for its integration of Zen and the arts.

name: Belmont Zen Center
address: North Market Street, St. Clairsville, OH 43950

name: Dharma Rain Zen Center
description: Dharma Rain Zen Center is a Soto Zen sangha in Portland, Oregon, that moved to a larger campus featuring a renovated church building and sustainable gardens. Founded by Gyokuko Carlson and Kyogen Carlson, it offers daily zazen, monthly sesshins, classes, and a residential training program. The center is known for its commitment to environmental sustainability and social justice.

name: Foundation for the Preservation of the Mahayana Tradition
Great Vow Zen Monastery
description: Great Vow Zen Monastery is a Soto Zen residential monastery founded by Chozen and Hogen Bays in rural northwestern Oregon. It offers year-round residential training, sesshin retreats, and programs that blend traditional Zen with contemporary practice. The monastery sits on 100 acres of forest and meadow along the Columbia River.

name: Kwan Um School of Zen (Providence Zen Center)
description: The Providence Zen Center is the head temple of the Kwan Um School of Zen, an international Korean Zen organization founded by Zen Master Seung Sahn. Located on 50 acres in Cumberland, Rhode Island, it features a traditional Korean-style dharma hall. The center offers daily practice, retreats, and a full residential training program.

name: Chua Lien Huu Tinh Do Temple
DFW Buddhist Vihara Texas Buddhist Meditation Center
Dawn Mountain Tibetan Temple
description: Dawn Mountain is a Tibetan Buddhist temple and community center in the Montrose neighborhood of Houston, founded by Lama Lhanang Rinpoche and Anne Klein (Rigzin Drolma). It offers weekly meditation, Buddhist philosophy courses, and retreat programs rooted in the Nyingma and non-sectarian Rime traditions. The center serves as a bridge between academic Buddhist studies and contemplative practice.

name: Fo Guang Shan Chung Mei Temple
Fo Guang Shan Xiang Yun Temple
address: 6720 N Capital of Texas Hwy, Austin, TX 78731
description: Fo Guang Shan Xiang Yun Temple is a Chinese Buddhist temple in Austin, Texas, affiliated with the international Fo Guang Shan organization. The temple offers meditation classes, dharma talks, cultural events, and traditional Buddhist ceremonies. It serves as a community hub for Chinese Buddhists in central Texas while promoting Humanistic Buddhism to a diverse audience.

name: Fo Guang Shan Xiang Yun Temple;International Buddhist Progress Society
address: North Capital of Texas Highway, Austin, TX 78731

name: Kadampa Meditation Center Texas
description: Kadampa Meditation Center Texas offers a variety of meditation classes, workshops, and retreats in the New Kadampa Tradition. Based in the Dallas-Fort Worth metropolitan area, it provides accessible Buddhist meditation instruction for beginners and experienced practitioners alike. The center offers regular drop-in classes, day courses, and longer retreat programs.

name: Karma Kagyu Tibetan Buddhist Meditation Center
Lhakang Building
address: West University Avenue, El Paso, Texas 79968

name: Ling Yen Mountain Temple
description: Ling Yen Mountain Temple is a large Chinese Pure Land Buddhist temple in the Houston metropolitan area. The ornate temple complex features traditional Chinese architecture and offers meditation sessions, chanting services, dharma classes, and cultural events. It serves the growing Chinese Buddhist community in Texas while welcoming visitors of all backgrounds.

name: Nichiren Buddhist Temple
address: Orchard Place, Salt Lake City, Utah 84101

name: Wat Lao Buddhavong
description: Wat Lao Buddhavong is one of the most prominent Lao Buddhist temples in the United States, featuring elaborate traditional Lao architecture and ornamental gardens. Located in rural northern Virginia, it serves the large Lao diaspora community in the Washington D.C. metropolitan area. The temple hosts cultural festivals, meditation retreats, and traditional ceremonies throughout the year.

name: Karme Choling
description: Karme Choling is a contemplative retreat center in the Shambhala tradition, set on 540 acres of rolling Vermont countryside. Founded by Chogyam Trungpa Rinpoche in 1970, it was one of the first Tibetan Buddhist centers in North America. The center offers meditation retreats, contemplative arts programs, and personal retreat opportunities year-round.

name: Shambhala Meditation Center of White River
Upper Valley Zen Center
address: Bridge Street, White River Junction, VT 05001

name: Dai Bai Zan Cho Bo Zen Ji
Pacific Hermitage
description: Pacific Hermitage is a small Thai Forest Tradition monastery in the Columbia River Gorge, a branch monastery of Abhayagiri. Situated in a rural area with views of Mount Adams, it supports a small community of monks in the Ajahn Chah lineage. The hermitage offers Sunday gatherings and periodic retreats while maintaining a focus on secluded monastic practice.

name: Rissho Kosei-Kai Buddhist Learning Center
address: Pacific Highway South, Federal Way, WA 98003

name: Sravasti Abbey
description: Sravasti Abbey is the only Tibetan Buddhist training monastery for Western nuns and monks in the United States, founded by Venerable Thubten Chodron. Located on 300 acres near the Idaho border, it operates as a residential monastic community emphasizing study, practice, and service. The abbey is known for its accessible online teachings and Dharma Friendship Foundation programs.

name: Milwaukee Zen Center
description: Milwaukee Zen Center is a Soto Zen practice community in the Katagiri Roshi lineage, offering daily meditation, monthly sesshins, and Buddhist study groups. Located in a residential neighborhood on the east side of Milwaukee, it maintains a small zendo and library. The center provides an accessible entry point to Zen practice in the upper Midwest.

name: Bhavana Society
address: Rt. 1 Box 218-3, Back Creek Rd, High View, WV 26808
description: The Bhavana Society is a forest monastery and meditation retreat center in the mountains of West Virginia, founded by Bhante Henepola Gunaratana, author of 'Mindfulness in Plain English.' It offers silent meditation retreats in the Theravada tradition on 70 acres of secluded Appalachian forest. The monastery supports both monastic residents and visiting meditators.

name: Casa de Dharma Uruguay
description: Casa de Dharma is a Tibetan Buddhist center in Montevideo offering meditation classes and dharma teachings in the Kagyu and Nyingma traditions. It serves the small but growing Buddhist community in Uruguay and hosts visiting teachers from the international Tibetan Buddhist community. The center provides accessible Buddhist practice in the Southern Cone.
